def: adj. (f. naʼī), New, fresh, novel; recent; modern; — strange
more: adj. S. नव; Pālī, navo, navīn, navā , P. nau , Z. nuk, navā . See alag (14, 15). 1. New; fresh; novel; recent. 1. Nayā dānā, nayā pānī . Prov. New grain, fresh water. (Said of wanderers). 2. Pahle do kān the, ab aur hue chār naë, Saiṅkṛoṅ phirte haiṅ chau-goshyā ṭopī-vāle . Whilom their caps two-eared, now four-eared caps they wear, Hundreds now strut in caps four-cornered every where. 3. Naī ghuṛyā dāṅt dikhāve . Boys. The new mare shows her teeth (bites and kicks). 2. Strange; new. Naë lachchhan. nayā purānā h. , v. n. 1. To cease to be new or strange. Woh to naë purāne bhī ho gaë . 2. To lose the zest of novelty. nayā taë tarad, nayā nikor , Spick and spannew. nayā k. , v. n. 1. To renew; restore. 2. To taste for the first time. 3. To burn (cloth). Wom. Zarā sī der meṅ aṅgarkhā nayā kar-ke dhar dīyā . Hin. Wom. (It is held unlucky to say jal gayā burned). navīn avasthā , n. f. Nonage;…
adj. New. نئيي سرسي naye sir se, adj. Anew, afresh. ¶ naiyā (from नौ ناو A boat) s. m. A boatman, ferryman.
subs: v.n. To cease to be new or strange; to become old or stale; — to lose the zest of novelty

v.t. To make new; to renew; renovate; restore; — to eat the first-fruits of; — to taste for the first time; — to burn (cloth, &c., — a euphemistic expression used by women instead of jalānā, or jal-jānā)
adj. Under five years old, rising five (a horse)

or naʼe-sir, or naye-sir-se, or naʼe-sire-se, adv. From the beginning; — over again; anew, afresh, de novo (syn. az sar-ě-nau); — naʼe-sire-se janam lenā, v.n. To be born again; — to obtain a new lease of life.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀡𑀯𑀺𑀅 (ṇavia), from Sanskrit नविय (náviya), नव्य (návya) (whence Bhojpuri नया (nayā)), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *náwyas, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *náwyas (whence Kamkata-viri nuĩ), from Proto-Indo-European *néwyos (whence English new). Doublet of نو (no). Cognates include Punjabi نَوَاں / ਨਵਾਂ (navā̃) and Nepali नयाँ (nayā̃).

def: s.m. def: s.f. Sight, vision, view; look, regard, glance; observation, inspection; supervision; — favourable regard, favour, countenance; — view, opinion, estimation; — intent, design; — regard, relation, reference; — influence of an evil eye; — doubt, uncertainty, perplexity
more: n. f. See اُنکهـ 2 to 4. 1. Sight. 2. Supervision. Jo arsh se hai farsh talak ādmī meṅ hai, Dekh āṅkh khol-ke kyā kyā nahīṅ hai is meṅ! Sab kuchh isī meṅ hai, par chāhiye nazar . Nazīr. From Earth to Heaven whatever is, is found in Man, Open your eyes and see how wonderful the plan! All things in him subsist, needs only eyes to scan. 3. Countenance; favor; deference. 4. Opinion; estimation. 5. Intent; design. 6. Relation; reference. 7. The influence of an evil eye. Nazar lagnā . nazar ānā , v. n. To come in sight; to appear. Bhaṅg jab chaṛhtī hai kyā hī mazā dikhlātī hai! Makkhiyāṅ uṛtī haiṅ aur īṅṭ nazar ātī hai! How jolly you must feel when you 're with Bhaṅgaṛs. bhang well plied, When flies swarm in the air and bricks by fancy spied! nazar-andāz k. , To reject. nazar-andāzī , n. f. Valuation of land or estimate of a crop upon inspection. nazar-bāz , n. m. 1. A detective. 2. An ogler. …

s. f. 1. Sight, vision, look, regard, a glance, view, inspection, observation. 2. Doubt, perplexity. نظر آنا naz̤ar ānā, To come to sight, to appear. نظر انداز naz̤ar-andāz, adj. s. m. Name of an herb, basil. (Ocimum pilosum: Roxb. ).

ety: Zend nazda, rt. nah = S. नह्; cf. नद्ध
def: adv. Near, &c. (i.q. nazdīk, q.v.). adv. Near (to, -ke), hard (by), close (to or by), not far (from), adjoining, contiguous (to); — on the point (of); almost; about; — in the opinion (of), in the estimation (of); — in the possession (of), with, by
more: Near, hard by, with, close to, neighbouring, contiguous, almost, about.
subs: (-ke), To draw near (to), to approach, &c.; coivit.

ety: Pārsī nazdīk; Zend nazdīg, or nazdeśt; Zand nazdista, superlative of nazd; cf. S. नेदिष्ठ, superlat. of नेद
def: adv. Near (to, -ke), hard (by), close (to or by), not far (from), adjoining, contiguous (to); — on the point (of); almost; about; — in the opinion (of), in the estimation (of); — in the possession (of), with, by
more: adv. 1. Near ( pās ). 2. In one's view. nazdīk honā yā jānā , v. n. 1. To approach; draw near. 2. To have sexual intercourse with. Uske nazdīk hūṅ, bahut hai dūr . Wom. Jān Sāhab.
Near, hard by, with, close to, neighbouring, contiguous, almost, about.
subs: (-ke), To draw near (to), to approach, &c.; coivit.

def: s.f. Nearness, proximity; — vicinity neighbourhood; propinquity; — approximation; approach; — coition.
more: n. f. 1. Proximity ( qurbat ). 2. Propinquity. 3. Coition.
1. s. f. Propinquity, nearness, proximity, approximation, approach, vicinity. 2. One near (in blood), a relative.

def: obscene, immoral
wety: From نا (nā, “not; imm; un”) + زیبا (zebā, “befitting, graceful”).

ety: v. adj. fr. نظف; see naz̤āfat
def: adj. Clean, pure; neat.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نظیف (nazīf), from Arabic نَظِيف (naẓīf).

def: adj. Ripe (as fruit, &c.), mature; — well-cooked (meat, &c.); soft
more: (from نضج) adj. Ripe, mature, cooked; soft.

def: adv. (Dakh.) corr. of nazdīk, q.v. adv. Near (to, -ke), hard (by), close (to or by), not far (from), adjoining, contiguous (to); — on the point (of); almost; about; — in the opinion (of), in the estimation (of); — in the possession (of), with, by
subs: (-ke), To draw near (to), to approach, &c.; coivit.

def: adv. (colloq. & Dakh.) corr. of nazdīk, q.v. adv. Near (to, -ke), hard (by), close (to or by), not far (from), adjoining, contiguous (to); — on the point (of); almost; about; — in the opinion (of), in the estimation (of); — in the possession (of), with, by
more: prepos. (dakh. for نزديک nazdīk, q. v.) Near, &c.
subs: (-ke), To draw near (to), to approach, &c.; coivit.

ety: act. part. of نزل 'to descend, alight,' &c.
def: part. adj. Descending, alighting; dismounting; arriving (at)
more: adj. Descending; alighting. nāzil h. , v. n. To descend or come upon; to befal.
part. act. (of نزل Heb. and Aram. ) 1. Descending. 2. Dismounting, alighting. نازل هونا nāzil honā, To descend, to alight.
subs: (-par), To descend (on), to alight (on, or at); to arrive (at); to come (upon), to befall.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نَازِل (nāzil), from Arabic نَازِل (nāzil).

ety: for A. نازلة nāzilat, fem. of nāzil
def: s.m. A disaster, calamity, misfortune (as descending from heaven); — a defluxion of humours, rheum; a disorder.
more: (part. act. fem. of نزل) s. m. A misfortune, disorder, calamity.

ety: act. part. of نظم 'to join; to arrange,' &c.
def: s.m. A composer, arranger, adjuster; organizer; regulator; administrator; governor; ruler; the chief officer of a province; — a composer of verses, a poet (i.q. nāz̤im-ě-ashʻār; — Pers. pl. nāz̤imān).
more: n. m. نظم strung together. 1. An administrator; a governor; the superior officer or governor. 2. A poet.
(part. act. of نظم) s. m. 1. A composer, arranger, adjuster. 2. A poet, composer of verses. 3. A governour, a ruler. (Pers. plur. ناظمان).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ناظم (nāzim), from the active participle of Arabic نَظَمَ (naẓama, “to organize; to compose”).

def: s.f. The office of a nāz̤im; — government, &c. (i.q. niz̤āmat, the more chaste form).

part. adj. & s.m. Seeing, looking, regarding, observing, inspecting; — a beholder, spectator, observer; an inspector, a superintendent, supervisor; keeper; commissary, an officer employed in a judicial court, a sheriff; a bailiff (i.q. nāz̤ir-ě-ʻadālat).
more: n. m. نظر saw. 1. An inspector; a supervisor. 2. ( nāzir-i-adālat ). A sheriff; bailiff. nazār'at , n. f. The office of a nāzir .

part. act. (of نظر Heb. and Aram. for ) 1. Seeing, looking, regarding, observing, inspecting, beholder. (Plur. ناظرين Beholders, spectators). 2. s. m. An inspector, superintendent, a guard, keeper, supervisor, observer. 3. An officer employed in a judicial court superiour to all peons, bailiffs, &c. a sheriff.

def: adj. Like, resembling, similar (to); equal (to); — s.m. & f. A like, a similitude, a parallel; an example, instance, a specimen, a case in point; a precedent
more: n. f. A precedent (مثال 4). nazīr denā yā lānā , v. n. 1. To cite; quote. 2. To adduce a precedent or authority.
(from نظر) s. m. f. Example, instance, an equal. adj. Alike, resembling, equal to. نظير السمت naz̤īru-s-samt, The Nadir.
subs: or naz̤īr lānā (-kī), To adduce an instance (of), or a precedent (for), to cite a case in point; to quote

ety: for A. ناظرة nāz̤irat, fem. of nāz̤ir
def: s.m. The eye; sight, vision; — s.m. & adv. Reading, studying (in opp. to repeating by heart; — e.g. nāz̤ira paṛhtā hai yā ḥifz̤)
more: adv. Reading a book, esp. the Korān. Nādrā paṛhtā hai yā hifz?
(part. act. fem. of نظر) s. m. 1. The eye, sight, vision. 2. Reading, studying. ناظره خواني nāz̤ira-ḵẖẉān-ī, Reading (in opposition to repeating by heart).

def: s.f. Inspection, supervision, superintendence; — office or duty of a nāz̤ir.
(obl. pl. of nāẓir, q.v.), s.m. pl. Beholders, spectators; — readers (of a newspaper), 'our readers.'
more: n. m. pl. 1. Beholders; spectators. 2. Readers; 'our readers.'

def: s.m. Ripening (as fruit, &c.); suppuration; — cooking, being nearly ready (as meat dressing).
more: (or naẓj ) s. m. Ripening, cooking; suppuration (of a wound).

ety: for A. نزلة nazlat, &c., n. of un. fr. نزل 'to descend,' &c.
def: s.m. Descent or defluxion of humours, rheum, catarrh
more: n. m. نزل descended. A catarrh. nazlā-band , A plaster on the temples for catarrh.
(from نزل Heb. and Aram. To flow; as Catarrh from καταῤῥέω ) s. m. A defluxion of humours, rheum, a catarrh.
subs: s.m. A plaster (on the temple) for catarrh.

ety: inf. n. of نظم 'to arrange; to join,' &c.
def: s.m. Order, arrangement, regulation, &c. (i.q. niz̤ām); — stringing (pearls, or beads); — composing (verses); — a string (of pearls, &c.); — poetry, verse
more: n. f. Poetry; verse. nazm o nasq , 1. Administration; management. 2. System of Government; policy. nazm o nasq k. , v. a. To organize; administer.
s. f. 1. Poetry, verse. 2. Order, arrangement. 3. A string. نظم ونسق naz̤m-o-nasaḳ, Order and arrangement.
subs: v.t. To turn into verse, to versify
s.m. Order and arrangement; organization; management; administration; — system of government, policy
(-kā), To put in order; to organize; to administer.
ex: قرآن مجید لفظ بلفظ آنحضرت صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہٰ وسلم کے قلب پر القا ہوا تھا اور وہی الفاظ اور اسی نظم سے جس طرح القا ہوئے تھے آنحضرت صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہٰ وسلم نے لوگوں کو پڑھ سنایا۔

The Holy Qur'an was inculcated in the heart of the Holy Prophet, peace and blessings of Allah be upon him, word for word, and in the same words and in the same order as they were inculcated, were recited by the Holy Prophet, peace and blessings of Allah be upon him, to the people.
میں لکچر سے پہلے تبرکاً اپنی نظم پڑھ لیا کرتا ہوں اگرچہ وہ نظم بودی پُھسپُھسی اور نامربوط سی ہوتی ہؠ۔
I [tend] to read my poetry before my lecture, although that [piece of] poetry usually [tends] to be weak, mild and inconsistent.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نَظْم (nazm), from Arabic نَظْم (naẓm). First attested as Middle Hindi نظم (nz̤m), from the Arabic root ن ظ م (n ẓ m). See other Urdu terms derived from the Arabic root ن ظ م.

def: direct plural of نَظْم (nazm)
s.m. A composer; an arranger, adjuster; — a poet.
more: s. m. 1. A composer, arranger, adjuster. 2. A poet.

def: oblique plural of نَظْم (nazm)
n. f. 1. A slim delicate woman. 2. A belle. 3. A sweetheart.

s.f. A vow; an offering, anything offered or dedicated; a gift or present (from an inferior to a superior); a fee paid to the State or to its representative on succeeding to an office or to property; — an interview (see the syn. bheṭ)
more: n. f. 1. An offering (H. chaṛhāvā ); a present from an inferior ( bheṭ , 3). 2. A fee paid to the state, or to its representative, on succeeding to an office or property. nazar denā, guzrānnā, yā k .; Sant. samangā , v. a. To tender; present; make an offering; sacrifice; offer. nazar mānnā , v. a. To vow; to make an offering. nazar o niyāz , n. f. Gifts and oblations. nazar h. , v. n. To be sacrificed; to be a prey to.

(Heb. and Aram. ) s. f. 1. A vow. 2. A gift (especially offered to a superiour, any thing dedicated), present, an offering. 3. ( met. ) Interview. نذرايمه naẕr-i-aʼimma, A charitable gift or grant for the support of priests, &c. a benefice, charity lands.
subs: s.f. A charitable gift or grant for the support of imāms or priests, &c.; a benefice; charity lands
or naẕr guẕrānnā (-ko), To present as an offering; to give as a present (to a superior)
v.t. To vow; to make an offering of, to offer, &c. (i.q. naẕr guẕrānnā); to sacrifice, to devote
v.t. To vow; to make an offering of
v.n. To be devoted or sacrificed (to or for); — to be or become a prey (to).

ety: naẕr, q.v.+āna = Zend ana = S. अन
def: s.m. A gift, or present (offered or received, when people of rank meet, or pay their respects to a prince); — the fee paid to government as an acknowledgment for a grant of land or any public office, &c. (i.q. naẕr).
more: s. m. 1. Gifts, &c. offered or received when people of rank meet or pay their respects to a prince. 2. A kind of tribute.

def: adj. Thin, slender, slim, delicate, tender, fragile; fine; light; brittle; nice; neat; elegant; genteel; subtle; — facetious; gracious; keen; sensitive, touchy, testy; — s.m. A species of jujube (i.q. nāzuk badan)
more: adj. 1. Thin; fine; light; brittle; fragile. 2. (Hin. Poet. nājo ) Delicate; slim. 1. Nāzuk badan . 2. Chār khet gajre ke khā gaī, nājuk peṭ bichārī kā! Song. Ṭesū . Four fields of carrots eaten up all, his stomach is so small! 3. Keen; sensitive. nāzuk, nāzuk-badan , n. m. A jujube; Zizyphus jujuba. nāzuk jagah , n. m. A vital part. nāzuk khayāl , n. m. 1. A fine thought. 2. Delicate perception; nice judgment. 3. One who has fine thoughts. nāzuk-mizāj, yā dimāg/?/ , 1. A sensitive mind. 2. A testy or touchy disposition. nāzuk muāmalah , A delicate matter. Adālat kā baṛā nāzuk muāmlah hai . Prov. Saying. nāzuk vaqt, yā zamānā , n. m. A critical moment; a crisis.

adj. 1. Thin, light, subtile, delicate, nice, tender, brittle, fragile, elegant. 2. Facetious, gracious, genteel. نازک ادا nāzuk-adā, adj. Delicate in performing or singing. نازک اداٴي nāzuk-adā-ī, s. f. Delicacy of performance. نازک خيال nāzuk-ḵẖayāl, adj. Of delicate or nice ideas. نازک خيالي nāzuk-ḵẖa yāl-ī, s. f. Delicateness of idea. نازک بدن nāzuk-badan, 1. Delicate. (Epithet of a mistress). 2. A species of jujube. (Zizyphus jujuba). نازک بدني nāzuk-badan-ī, s. f. Delicacy of form or body. نازک مزاج nāzuk-mizāj, Delicate in constitution or complexion. نازک مزاجي nāzuk-mizāj-ī, s. f. Delicacy of complexion.
subs: adj. Having sweet notes (as the nightingale, &c.); delicate in performing or singing
s.f. Sweetness of note; delicacy of performance or singing
adj. Of delicate frame, delicate (epithet of a mistress); — s.m. A species of jujube, Zizyphus jujuba
s.f. Delicacy of body or of form
s.f. A tender part or spot; a vital part
s.m. A fine thought; delicate perception; nice judgment; — adj. & s.m. Having a fine perception; of delicate or nice ideas; — one who has a fine perception, &c.
s.m. A delicate or sensitive brain; a sensitive mind; — adj. & s.m. Having (or one who has) a sensitive brain or mind; — a touchy or testy person

s.m. A delicate constitution; — a sensitive disposition, or temper; — adj. & s.m. Of a delicate complexion or habit of body; — of a sensitive disposition or temper; — a person of delicate constitution or complexion, &c.; — a person of a touchy or testy disposition (syn. nāzuk-damāg̠)
s.f. Delicacy of complexion or habit of body, &c.
s.m. A delicate affair; a ticklish matter
or nāzuk zamāna, s.m. A critical moment; a crisis.
ex: She is a very delicate girl.
I am in a really fragile condition.

def: s.f. Tenderness, softness, delicacy; nicety, &c. (see nāzuk).
more: n. f. Softness; tenderness; delilcacy; nicety.
s. f. Delicacy, nicety, delicateness, tenderness, &c.

ety: v. adj. fr. نزّ 'to run with rapidity'; 'to exude,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Brisk, light, active; — sharp, acute, intelligent, ingenious, witty; — running from place to place (an ostrich); — (water) springing up; — a brisk or active man, &c.; — an ostrich which keeps moving about without settling in any one place; — water springing from the ground, or land from which water exudes.
more: s. 1. An intelligent, discerning or active man. 2. Land from which water exudes, or water springing from the ground. 3. An ostrich which keeps moving about without settling in any one place.

ety: intens. n. fr. naz̤m, or niz̤ām, qq.v.
def: s.m. A governor, an administrator, a ruler, a prince or lord; the title of the prince of Hyderabad in the Deccan; — a stringer (of pearls); composer (of verses).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نظام (nizām), from Arabic نِظَام (niẓām).

r: naz̤z̤āra, naz̤āra, naz̤z̤ārat, naz̤z̤ār, naz̤ar, naz̤āra-bāz, naz̤āre-bāz

more: n. m. Amorous glances; ogling. nazāre-bāz , n. m. One who ogles. nazārah k., yā mārnā , v. n. To ogle; wanton with one's eyes.
s. m. Sight, view, show, inspection. نظاره باز naz̤āra-bāz, An ogler. نظاره کن naẕẕāra-kun, Gazing, surveying.
subs: or naz̤āra mārnā (-par ?), To cast amorous glances (at), to ogle; to wanton with the eyes
or naz̤āra-kun, adj. & s.m. Gazing (at), surveying; — gazer, &c.

def: s.f. Seeing, looking at; sight; observation; — s.m. Beholder, spectator (pl. naz̤z̤āragiyān).
more: s. f. Seeing, looking at, observation. s. m. Beholder, spectator.

ety: for orig. le, Ap. Prk. लइए, or लहिए; S. लब्धे
def: postp. of the agent or active case in High Hindī, and Urdū (orig. a dialec. dat. affix of W. Hindī).
more: n. f. 1. A reed ( nalī , 1). 2. A pipe; flute. 3. The pipe of a huqqa.
A particle affixed to the nominative of a transitive verb in the past tense.

def: adv. No, not; — nay; — ne — ne, Neither — nor.
more: adv. 1. No, not, neither, nor. 2. Nay.
A particle affixed to the name of the agent with a transitive verb in a past tense.

def: s.m. pl. (of naʻl, q.v.), Shoes, &c.
more: s. plur. of نعل naﻌl, q. v.

def: s.m. pl. (of něʻmat, q.v.), Benefits, favours; — comforts, blessings (of life); — pleasure, comfort, affluence, ease.
more: s. plur. (of نعمت Heb. ) 1. Favours, benefits. 2. Pleasure, comfort, affluence, ease or blessing (of life). ¶ نعم البدل niﻌma-l-badal, Blessings of the change, excellent the change! change for the better.

def: s.f. (dialec.) = nićhāwar, q.v. s.m. A propitiatory offering, a sacrifice; a victim; — money which is scattered at marriages and on other festive occasions (i.q. nis̤ār, q.v.); a present or offering (to a great personage); — the price paid for an idol
s.m. The tree Alangium hexapetalum.

ety: prob for newa = Zend naêma; S. नेम; but cf. S. नीवि

more: (prop. نيفه) s. m. A breeches belt, the part of the drawers through which the string runs to tie them round the loins with.

ety: prob for newa = Zend naêma; S. नेम; but cf. S. नीवि

more: n. m. The part of the drawers through which the string runs; a seam.

ety: prob. S. नियम (Prk. नेअम; H. nem)+कः; — or Prk. नियगं; S. निज+कं
def: s.m. Customary presents on festive occasions (i.q. neg); — neg-jog-wāle (fem. -wāliyāṅ), s.m. pl. People claiming wedding gifts
more: n. m. 1. Custom; usage; rule. 2. A privilege; exclusive right. Kuchh tumhārā hī neg nahīṅ hai . 3. ( neg jog ) A present to relatives and dependants on festive occasions. 1. Negī tīyal apan bagaṛ māṅ ghar ghar jāë dikhāūṅgī . Rus. song. The new suit I've a present had in every house I'll show off glad. 2. Jo saë chīj rīt negan kī, us nū beg sambhālo rī! Banvāṛā nauṅdan chālo rī! Rus. Marriage song. The usual presents quickly now provide, The bridegroom's party hither hasting stride! 3. Bandharvār baṅdhā nāyan sūṅ! Soph rupaiyā lā de nā! Jachchā! jach neg chukā de nā! Rus. Jachchāgīrī . Laurel festoons the barber's wife hath hung, Let clothes and silver now 'mongst us be flung! O mother new! our presents give, we 've waited long! neg denā , v. a. To give the customary presents. neg k. , v. n. To begin (a work) at an auspicious moment. neg'ī , adj. Rightful; having a claim. neg jog-wāliān …
s. f. Presents at marriages and on other festive occasions made to relations and to particular servants, and considered by them as perquisites which they are entitled to.
subs: (-ko), To give the customary presents (at a wedding, &c.)
To begin (a work) at an auspicious moment
To be made good use of (in the last two compounds neg seems to be a corr. of the P. nek).

ety: neg, q.v. + ī = Prk. इओ = S. इकः (इन्+कः)
def: s.m. One who claims or expects the customary present or fee at a marriage or on any other festive occasion (as a village servant, or a tenant, or dependant, &c.)
Tenants and dependants, to whom it is customary to give the neg, q. v.
s. f. 1. Affection, kindness, love, friendship. 2. Oil.

ety: Prk. नेहो and नेहओ; S. स्नेहः and स्नेह+कः
def: Oiliness; — oil; grease; — tenderness, love, affection, kindness, friendship
more: n. m. S. स्नेह Pr. neho . Love (سينه). Nainan ko neh na ṭūṭe, jaise bel birachh ko lipṭe, sūkh jāë na chhūṭe . Rus. song. Love parts not from the eyes, as a creeper clasps a tree, Its hold 'twill not let go though withered up it be. nehā lagnā , v. n. To form an attachment. Umanḍ ghumanḍ kar voh jo āyā, Andar maiṅ ne palang bichhāyā, Merā vā kā lāgā neh: E sakhī, sājan?' 'Nā, sakhī, meh' . Riddle. Makrī . He, coming in, such dash displayed, Perforce the bed I inside made, Love soon arose betwixt us twain: 'Your lover, dear?' 'No, dear, the rain.'
subs: or nehā lagnā (-kā), To feel affection (for); to form an attachment.

ety: Prk. नेहो and नेहओ; S. स्नेहः and स्नेह+कः
def: Oiliness; — oil; grease; — tenderness, love, affection, kindness, friendship
subs: or nehā lagnā (-kā), To feel affection (for); to form an attachment.

s.f. (dialec.) = leju, q.v.

def: adj. & adv. Good, virtuous; beautiful; excellent; — lucky; — well; — very, exceedingly; — s.m.(?) Anything good

adj. & s.m. Meaning well, well-disposed; benevolent, friendly, affectionate; — fortunate; — a well-wisher, well-disposed man, &c.
adj. Just, virtuous, of good actions

adj. & s.m. & f. Of good or happy lot, fortunate, happy, blest; — of good disposition, well-behaved, well-disposed, good, true; dutiful, obedient; — a fortunate person; a fortunate or happy wife; a well-disposed or good man or woman

s.m., or nek ćalanī, s.f. Good conduct
s.m. Increased pay for good conduct, or good-conduct pay
s.m., or nek-ćalanī kī dhārī, s.f. Good-conduct stripe
or nek-ḵẖaṣlat, or nek-ḵẖulq, or nek-ḵẖo, adj. & s.m. Of good habits; of good disposition, good-natured, humane; well-bred; — well-disposed person, good-natured man
or nek-ḵẖulqī, or nek-ḵẖoʼī, s.f. Well-disposedness; good-naturedness
adj. & s.m. Well-wishing, benevolent, affectionate, friendly, faithful, grateful; — a well-wisher, &c.
adj. Having a clear conscience; — pious, devout
adj. & s.m. Of good breed or temper, well-bred; of good disposition, good-natured
adj. & s.m. Of good conduct, well-conducted; — a well-conducted person
s.f. A fortunate moment, happy juncture; an auspicious occasion
s.f. Good understanding or sense; goodness of intellect
adj. Beneficent; of good habits or disposition
s.m. A good name; celebrity; — adj. Having a good name, of good character or repute; famous, renowned, celebrated
s.f. Good name; good character; reputation
or sanad, s.f. A certificate of good character
or nek-nāmī ḥāṣil karnā, To earn or win a good name or reputation
adj. Of good disposition, well-disposed, good-natured
s.f. Goodness of intention; good faith
adv. With good intentions; in good faith

ety: prob. S. नेम+कः; or corr. fr. S. कणीकः; cf. nikkā
def: adj. & adv. Little, small; a little; — for a little while, for a short time.
more: Rus. adj. Cont. of tanak . A little ( thoṛā , 1). Āo nek yahāṅ, Naṅd rānī! apne lāl ke kautak dekho! Dūdhe milāvat pānī! Song.

adj. Good; virtuous. nek anjām . A happy ending. nek-bakht , adj. 1. Fortunate; lucky. 2. Dutiful; obedient. nek-bakht , n. f. The wife so-called. nek-bakhtī , n. f. 1. Good fortune. 2. Goodness. nek-chalan, yā ravaiyā , n. m. Good conduct. nek-chalanī , n. f. Good conduct. nek-chalnī kā izāfa tankhuāh , Good conduct pay. nek-chalnī kā billā yā dhārī , Good conduct stripes. nek-khaslat, zāt, sīrat, nīyat, yā nihād , adj. Well-disposed; good-natured. nek-nām , adj. Of good character or repute. nek-nāmī , n. f. Good name or reputation. nek-nāmī kī chiṭṭhī yā sanad . A certificate of character. nek nīyatī se , In good faith. nek o bad ; H. burā bhalā , Good and evil.

adj. 1. Good, lucky. 2. (subst.) Any thing good. نيک اختر nek-aḵẖtar, Fortunate. نيک اعتقادي nek-iﻌtikād-ī, s. f. Goodness of trust or reliance, good faith. نيک انديش nek-andesh, Meaning well, well disposed. نيک انديشي nek-andesh-ī, s. f. Good intention. نيک باز nek-bāz, Just, virtuous, of good actions. نيکبخت nek-baḵẖt, Fortunate (commonly meaning, of good dispositions). نيکبختي nek-baḵẖt-ī, s. f. Virtue, good fortune. نيک پاک nek-pāk, Virtuous. نيک تدبير nek-tadbīr, Good in counsel or management. نيک تن nek-tan, Sleek-skinned (a horse). نيک خصال يا خصلت يا خلق يا خو nek-ḵẖiṣāl or -ḵẖaṣlat or -ḵẖulḳ or -ḵẖo, Of good dispositions or habits, good-natured. نيک خواه nek-ḵẖẉāh, Well-wishing, benevolent, faithful, grateful. نيک ديانت nek-diyānat, Having a clear conscience. نيک ذات يا طينت nek-ẕāt or t̤īnat, Of good disposition. نيک ذاتي nek-ẕāt-ī, s. f. Goodness of disposition. نيک سيرت nek-s…

def: adj. (dakh.) for نيک خواه nekḵẖẉāh, q. v. under نيک nek.
s. m. (dakh.) Wishing well, good will, benevolence.
s.f. Goodness; good; piety, virtue; probity; — beauty, elegance
more: n. f. Opp. of badī . Goodness ( bhalāī , 1). 1. Nekī kā badlā badī . Prov. Evil for good. 2. Khudā tumheṅ nekī de! God bless you! 3. Nekī karo, Khudā se pāo! Prov. Do good and God will reward you! 4. Kāyā le kām, nekī le nām . Prov. With your body work, with goodness gain a name. 5. Ab nekī kā zamāna nahīṅ rahā . The age of goodness is no more. nekī o badī , Weal or wee.
s. f. Goodness, good, piety, virtue, probity, beauty.
subs: To do good (to a person, kisī-ke ḥaq-meṅ)
s.f. Good and evil; weal or woe.

def: adj. & adv. Good; — beautiful, elegant, handsome; — well; rightly; easily; excellently, elegantly
more: (in Hind. nīko ) adj. Good, beautiful, elegant; well. نيکوخدمتي nīko-ḵẖidmat-ī, Goodness of service. نيکوکار neko-kār, Of good actions, a beneficent person, benefactor. نيکوکاري neko-kār-ī, s. f. Beneficence, doing good.
subs: s.f. Goodness of service; good service
adj. & s.m. Of good actions, beneficent; — a beneficent person, a benefactor
s.f. Doing good, beneficence; benevolence. (N.B. neko may be compounded with other words, like nek, and with the same signification, e.g. nekū-nīyat = nek-nīyat.)

def: adj. (dialec. or Braj) = nek, 'little,' q.v.
subs: adv. Very little, just a little (i.q. nek-sā, thoṛā-sā).

def: s.m. (Braj) corr. of nāḵẖun, q.v. s.m. Nail (of the finger or toe); talon, claw
subs: v.t. To write with the finger-nail (considered an accomplishment)
or nāḵẖun-tarāsh, s.m. A small knife, or scissors, for paring the nails
v.t. To pare the nails; — to trip, or stumble (a horse)
v.n. To be in (one's) possession; to be lying in the pocket.

def: adj. (dialec. or Braj) = nek, 'little,' q.v.
subs: adv. Very little, just a little (i.q. nek-sā, thoṛā-sā).

def: nail polish, a cosmetic lacquer applied to the fingernails or toenails

ety: Prk. निअमो, नियमो, नेअमो, or नेयमो; S. नियमः
def: s.m. Rule, principle; religious observance; vow, &c. (i.q. niyam, q.v.)
more: n. m. S. नियम 1. ( nem dharam ) Religious observances. Jo chhoṛā hai nem ko kho dīyā tap kā mūl, Chhoṛ chhāṛ-ke phir līyā tā-ke moṅḍhe dhūl! Charan Dās. 2. A rule; principle. 3. A religious vow. nem bāṅdhnā, k., yā lenā , v. n. 1. To bind oneself to; to vow. 2. To observe; practice. nem-patr , n. m. A written agreement (اقرارنامه). nit-nem , n. m. Daily religious service or observances. nit-nem , adv. Punctually; every day.

s. m. 1. Vow, compact, agreement. 2. Any religious observance voluntarily practised; piety. نيم دهرم nem-dharm, s. m. Abstinence, good conduct. ¶ nīm ( निंब ) s. m. Name of a tree. (Melia azedirachta). Margosa tree: Herklots.
subs: or nem karnā, To make a strict rule (to), to bind oneself (to); to make a vow, to vow (that); — to observe, to practise
s.m. A written agreement or stipulation
s.m. A religious observance; fasting; abstinence; temperateness, good conduct
(-ko), To take as a rule, &c. (i.q. nem karnā, q.v.)
s.m. & adv. Daily religious duty or observance, &c.; — regularly, punctually, every day.

def: intj. Welcome! how good! most excellent
subs: Most excellent the change! — s.m. Change for the better.

def: s.m. (in H. also fem.), A foundation, &c. (i.q. neʼo, the pop. form, q.v.)

ety: for A. نعمة, fr. نِعْمَ, orig. نَعِمَ 'to be good; to be affluent, comfortable,' &c.
def: s.f. Comfort, convenience, ease; affluence, wealth; — graciousness, beneficence; — a benefit, favour, boon, blessing; — delight, joy; — a delicacy, dainty
more: n. f. 1. A blessing; boon; comfort. 2. Dainties; delicacies. nemat-i-gair-mutaraqqibah , An unlooked-for-benefit; a windfall.
s. f. 1. Favour, graciousness, benefit. 2. Delight, joy. 3. Affluence, ease, wealth, good thing or comfort of life, blessing or favour of heaven. 4. A delicacy.
subs: s.m. House or abode of affluence, &c.; — a palace; — paradise
or něʻmat ḵẖẉur, adj. Enjoying affluence or ease
An unlooked-for piece of good fortune; a windfall.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نِعْمَت (ni'mat), taken from Arabic نِعْمَة (niʕma).

def: s.f. (dialec.) = nićhāwar, q.v. s.m. A propitiatory offering, a sacrifice; a victim; — money which is scattered at marriages and on other festive occasions (i.q. nis̤ār, q.v.); a present or offering (to a great personage); — the price paid for an idol
s.m. The tree Dalbergia ougeinensis.
s.f. The circumference, or felly, or outer rim (of a wheel); edge, rim; — a windlass or framework (for the rope of a well)
more: adj. 1. Regular; punctual. Dātā ko dene kā dukh sau maṅgtoṅ ne gherā, Nemī nahāë, bahut dukh pāve, kīyā asnān saverā, Jo bhaje so sukhyā, piyāre . Kabir. The giver pain-giving the beggars in crowds all hem in, At bathing so punctual enduring much pain the dawn in, The worship of hearts is, beloved, what gives peace within. 2. Religious. 3. True to one's vows.
subs: or nemī-ćakra, s.m. Name of a prince descended from Parīkshit (he is said to have removed the capital of India to Kauśambi after the inundation of Hastinapur).

def: postp. (dialec.) = ne, q.v. postp. of the agent or active case in High Hindī, and Urdū (orig. a dialec. dat. affix of W. Hindī).
more: a particle, see S̱. ني ne. ¶ naiṅ (dakh.) Not: see نہين nahīṅ.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit नयन (nayana), from the root नी (nī), from Proto-Indo-European *neyH-.

def: s.m. (dialec.) = niyam, q.v. s.m. A sheath, scabbard, case (i.q. miyān, q.v.); — a bandage (for a broken limb), splint; — a plough-tail or handle.
s.m. The country of Nepal in the north of India.
more: n. prop. Name of a country.

def: s.f. Red arsenic.

s.m. An ornament, embellishment, decoration; attire; the costume of an actor; — the part of a stage behind the scenes (or rather behind the curtain which was stretched across the stage and served for scenes); the tiring-room of a stage.
adv. Slowly. Nepe nepe baglī chale, kahar ghāo machhlī par kare . The paddy bird moves but slowly, but he sorely wounds the fish when he makes his dart. Carnegy .
s.m. & f. = nūpur, q.v.
more: s. f. An ornament for the toes and feet or worn on the ankles. See نوپر.

def: adv. Near ( nazdīk ). Sājan āvat hūṅ suno kuchh neṛe, kuchh dūr, Palkan hī se jhāṛ lūṅ un pāvan kī dhūr . Dohā. My husband's coming home, his steps now far, now near I hear, I'll with my eyelash brush away the dust from his feet dear.
adv. = nere, or ner, q.v.
more: (see نيري nere ) adv. (dakh.) 1. Near. 2. Under the protection: see پناه panāh.

ety: prob. fr. neṛ = ner or niyar, q.v.
def: v.t. To keep near (one), or under (one's) care, to care for, to look after.
adv. (dialec.) = nere = niyar, q.v.
adv. = neṛe, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) Straw.

ety: prob. i.q. neza, z and sh being interchangeable
def: s.m. Sting (esp. of a venomous animal); — a lancet; a probe; — a puncture, a small incision
more: n. m. A sting. nesh-zanī , n. f. 1. Stinging. 2. Backbiting; maligning. 3. See bhāṅjī mārnā .

s. m. 1. Sting (of a venomous animal). 2. A puncture. 3. A lancet. نيش زن nesh-zan, Stinger; ( met. ) an incendiary, one who inflames disputes, a telltale. نيش زني nesh-zan-ī, s. f. Striking with a sting; exciting of quarrels, &c.
subs: adj. & s.m. Stinging; wounding, &c.; — a stinger; (met.) a backbiter, maligner; tell-tale; one who inflames disputes, an incendiary; a marplot
s.f. The striking with a sting, stinging; — backbiting, maligning; tale-bearing; the exciting of quarrels, mischief-making; — marring, frustrating.

def: s.m. A lancet, fleam (i.q. nishtar, or nashtar, q.v.). s.f. A lancet, fleam
subs: or nashtar lagānā (-ko, or -meṅ), To apply the lancet (to), to lance; to bleed, phlebotomize.

def: adj. Non-existent; null, void
more: adj. S. नास्ति non-existence. lit. non est . Null; void ( nā-būd ).

Nought, nonexistence. نيست کرنا nest karnā, To abolish, to annihilate, to ruin, to destroy. نيست نابود کرنا nest nā-būd karnā, To demolish, to annihilate, to destroy, to ruin. نيست نابود هونا nest nā-būd honā, To perish, to be annihilated, ruined, destroyed, &c.
subs: v.t. To bring to nought, to annihilate; to ruin, destroy, demolish; to dissipate, squander; to render null and void, to abolish, &c.
or nest-o-nābūd karnā, v.t. (intens.) = nest karnā, q.v.
or nest honā, v.n. To be annihilated, to be ruined, or destroyed, &c.; to perish; to become extinct.

def: s.f. Nullity, non-existence; — annihilation, ruin, destruction; — poverty, destitution
more: n. f. 1. Non-existence; nullity; annihilation. 2. Poverty. nestī-bharā , adj. 1. Unfortunate ( bad-bakht ). 2. Idle. nestī chhānā , v. n. To be in ruins.
subs: 'Ruin or desolation to overshadow,' to be in ruins.

def: s.m. Mucus of the nose, snot (syn. reṅṭā; poṅṭā).
adj. (dialec.) Left-handed.
s.m. (f. -trī), A leader, guide, conductor; a chief, master, owner.
more: Tir. n. m. Mucus from the nose; snot; snivel ( reṅṭ ).

def: a transliteration of the Hebrew surname נְתַנְיָהוּ (Netanyahu)
adv. Certainly, assuredly, undoubtedly (see nīṭh or neṭh).
Hin. adv. Certainly; undoubtedly.
s.f. A cord used to whirl round the churn-staff with (i.q. netr).
more: n. f. S. नेत्रं The cord with which the churn-staff is worked.
s. f. A cord used to whirl round the churn-staff with.

ety: for net = Prk. नित्तं; S. नित्यं
def: adv. (dialec.) = nit, or nitya, q.v. adj. & adv. Continual, incessant, perpetual, repeated, constant, invariable, eternal, endless, &c. (see nitya); — always, ever, daily, continually, unceasingly, perpetually, constantly, invariably; — s.m. The base of an obtuse-angled triangle
subs: or nit-nit, adv. intens. of nit, q.v.
s.m., or nit-kiriyā, s.f. Daily duty, or routine; constant or indispensable act or duty (as observance of the five great sacrifices, or any daily and necessary rite)
or nit-nayā, adj. Ever new, or fresh; — changeable, variable.

def: adv. Not in this manner, not thus.
s.m. The eye; — a symbolic expression for the number two; — the string with which a churning-stick is whirled round (i.q. netī); — the root of a tree
more: n. m. S. नेत्र The eyes. netr phūṭnā , v. n. To become blind.
subs: 'The eye to burst,' to become blind
s.m. Any disease of the eyes; ophthalmia
s.m. (f. -inī), A person afflicted with ophthalmia, or any other disease of the eyes
(˚tra+ut˚), s.m. (f. -ā), 'Eye-feast'; any pleasing or beautiful object
(˚tra+aush˚), s.m. Medicine for the eyes; collyrium; green sulphate of iron (used as collyrium)
(˚tra+au˚), s.f. A kind of drug, Odina pinnata

ety: perhaps netra = S. नियन्न्त्रित+lot, q.v.; or netr, 'eye'+lot
def: s.m. A prisoner.
s.m. = netā, q.v. s.m. (f. -trī), A leader, guide, conductor; a chief, master, owner.
s.m. Butter.
s.m. (dialec.) = newal, q.v. s.f. 1˚ = niwār or niwāṛ, q.v.; — 2˚ (dialec.) = newar, q.v. s.f. Coarse, broad tape (used to lace beds, and to border tents, &c, with)
s.m. (dialec.) 1˚ = neg, q.v.; — 2˚ = neh, q.v. s.m. Customary presents on festive occasions (i.q. neg); — neg-jog-wāle (fem. -wāliyāṅ), s.m. pl. People claiming wedding gifts
subs: (-ko), To give the customary presents (at a wedding, &c.)
To begin (a work) at an auspicious moment
To be made good use of (in the last two compounds neg seems to be a corr. of the P. nek).

def: s.f. Foundation (of a wall, &c.), basis
more: n. f. See بنياد (1). A foundation; basis. neo dharnā, ḍālnā, yā jamānā , v. a. To found; lay the foundation of. nīvā nās khonā , v. a. To destroy; to leave no trace of; to cut up root and branch.
s. f. The foundation (of a house, &c.). نيوڐالنا new ḍālnā, To found.
subs: or neʼo dharnā, or neʼo jamānā (-kī), To lay the foundation (of), to found.

def: s.m. (dialec.) Custom, usage, manner, practice; — likeness, similitude; — a proverb; maxim; motto.
more: n. m. 1. Custom; manner. 2. Likeness; similitude. 3. A proverb; motto.

ety: newāj(nā), corr. of nawāz(nā), q.v.+ū = Prk. इउओ = S. (इ)तृ+कः
def: s.m. (rustic or dialec.) Nourisher, cherisher, protector.
s.f. = newar, q.v. s.f. 1˚ = niwār or niwāṛ, q.v.; — 2˚ (dialec.) = newar, q.v. s.f. Coarse, broad tape (used to lace beds, and to border tents, &c, with)
more: s. f. A sore in the feet of a horse.
s. m. A weasel, a ferret, a mongoose. (Viverra mungo).

def: s.m. (f. -ī), A mungoose, an ichneumon, Viverra mungo, and V. ichneumon; — a ferret; a weasel.
v.t. = niwānā, or nawānā, q.v.
more: (caus. of نيونا) v. a. To bend.

def: s.f. A kind of flower.
more: s. f. Name of a flower.

def: s.f. 1˚ = niwār or niwāṛ, q.v.; — 2˚ (dialec.) = newar, q.v. s.f. Coarse, broad tape (used to lace beds, and to border tents, &c, with)
more: s. f. Tape. See نوار niwār.

ety: Prk. नेउरं; S. नूपुरं; — or Prk. नेवडी; S. म+नेर+ई
def: s.f. The ankle or pastern-joint (of a horse); — a sore on the pastern-joint (esp. that caused by cutting in going); — (dialec.) the sock put on to prevent a horse's cutting his pastern-joints
more: n. f. A sore in the foot of a horse, etc. nevar lagnā , v. n. A horse's cutting himself on one foot with his other shoe.
s. f. A sore in the feet of a horse.
subs: (-ko), To cut the pastern-joints in running (a horse).

def: s.m. A particular high caste of Brāhmaṇs; — man of that caste.
s.f. Arabian jasmine, Jasminum zambac; — Nyctanthes arbor tristis.

v.t. = niwārnā, q.v. v.t. To surround; to keep off, ward off; to exclude, to shut out; to protect, defend; — to prohibit, forbid, interdict; — to prevent, hinder, impede, obstruct, interrupt; to check, restrain; to oppose.
more: v. a. To shut out, to exclude.

def: s.m. = naut, or nautā, or neʼotā, qq.v.
v.t. = nautnā, q.v. v.t. To invite (to a feast or entertainment); — to promise a gift to.
v.t. = nautnā, q.v. v.t. To invite (to a feast or entertainment); — to promise a gift to.

s.f. = nemćhāwar = nićhāwar, q.v. s.f. (dialec.) = nićhāwar, q.v. s.m. A propitiatory offering, a sacrifice; a victim; — money which is scattered at marriages and on other festive occasions (i.q. nis̤ār, q.v.); a present or offering (to a great personage); — the price paid for an idol
s.m. A sore in the pastern-joint (of a horse; — i.q. newar, q.v.); — a cancer (Pers. syn. hazār-ćashma).
s.m. (f. -ī), A mungoose, an ichneumon, Viverra mungo, and V. ichneumon; — a ferret; a weasel.
more: n. m. S. नकुल 1. A weasel; ferret; mongoose; inchneumon; Viverra ichneumon . 2. ( Slang ) Membrum virile . neole kī phāṅsī , n. f. (Slang). Pudendum fœmina .
s. m. A weasel, a ferret, a mongoose. (Viverra mungo).

def: s.f. (dialec.) A purse.
v.n. (dialec.) = niwnā = nawnā, q.v.
more: v. n. To stoop, to bow.

def: s.m. An invitation, &c. (i.q. nautā or notā, q.v.)
more: see نوتا) s. m. An invitation, or the presents sent along with an invitation to the person invited, or made by a guest. See Qanoon-e-islam.
subs: s.m. One who receives an invitation, a guest.

ety: ne = nai or nāʼe, q.v.+za, dim. aff. = ća = ak = S. क; cf. Zend naêza
def: s.m. A piece of reed (from which pens are made); — a short spear, a lance, javelin, dart pike
more: n. m. 1. A lance (H. barchhī ) 2. A reed for pens. nezā-bāzī , n. f. Throwing the spear; tilting. nezā-bardār , n. m. A lancer ( bhālā-bardār ). nezā hilānā , v. n. To handle a spear.

s. m. 1. A spear, lance, javelin, dart, pike. 2. A piece of reed from which pens are made. نيزه باز neza-bāz, Playing with a spear, a spearman. نيزه بازي neza-bāz-ī, s. f. Throwing the spear, tilting. نيزي بردار neze-bardār, A spearman. نيزي دار neze-dār, Armed with a spear.
subs: s.f. A throwing of the spear; — jousting, tilting
vulg. neze-bardār, s.m. 'Spear-bearer,' a spearman; a lancer
vulg. neze-dār, adj. & s.m. Armed with a spear; — a spearman
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نیزَه (nēza). First attested in c. 1649 as Middle Hindi نیزہ (neza).

def: direct plural; oblique/vocative singular
oblique plural of نیزَہ (neza)
adj. (dialec.) = nannhā, q.v.
more: adj. (dakh.) Small, little, junior: see ننها nannhā.

(fr. nhannā, q.v.), s.m. & f. (Dakh.) A child.
s.f. Fleeing, flight
subs: s.f., or nhāṭā-nhāṭī, s.f. Flight; mutual flight.

def: v. a. (dakh. caus. of نهاٿنا nhāṭnā ) To cause to flee, to put to flight ( Harris ).

ety: nhāṭ˚ = Prk. एहट्ट(इ), fr. S. स्नुस्त, p.p.p. of rt. स्नुस्
def: v.n. (dialec.) To vanish, disappear, flee, run away; to abscond; to desert.
more: v. n. (dakh.) To flee, to run away, to betake oneʼs self to flight, to escape.

def: s.m. A runaway, fugitive; a deserter.
more: (from نهاٿنا) s. (dakh.) Runaway, deserter, fugitive ( Harris ).

def: s.m. (dialec.) = nāʼī, q.v. part. Having bent, or bowed, &c. (see nānā).
s. (dakh.) A mattress, quilt, bedding ( Harris ): see نہالچه nihālcha.
(dialec., Bhojpurī), A suffixed emphatic particle.
more: A negative prefix. Ni-gharā . Houseless. Ni-hattā . Unarmed.

def: An insep. prefix = ni, q.v. A negative or privative prefix (used euphonically for, or corrupted from, nir, nis, or nih̥), e.g. ni-ḍar, 'Fearless' (numerous instances of the use of this prefix will be found in the following pages).

A negative or privative prefix (used euphonically for, or corrupted from, nir, nis, or nih̥), e.g. ni-ḍar, 'Fearless' (numerous instances of the use of this prefix will be found in the following pages).
more: prep. In, into, within, on, upon. The reverse of निर् .

def: An insep. prep. or prefix (the reverse of nir, and of ut; it is positive and intensive, and denotes) 'in, into, within; down, under, back; on, upon.'
s.m. = nīmb, or nīm, qq.v. s.m. The tree Melia azadirachta (pop. nīmb, or nīm, q.v.)
subs: s.m. The coral-tree, Erythrina fulgens (supposed to be one of the trees of paradise)
s.f. The fruit or berry of the nīm tree.

ety: Prk. निव्वाहो; S. निर्वाहः; see nibāhnā
def: s.m. Carrying on or through, conducting, managing; bringing to an end; completion, end; — accomplishing, accomplishment, execution, performance (of), fulfilment, keeping a promise or engagement; success; keeping, guarding; supporting, maintaining, providing means (for); — support, maintenance, livelihood; supply, sufficiency (of), adequacy; accommodation; — spending, passing (time); doing (with, -se), making shift (with), bearing or putting up (with), tolerating; living in peace (with); — steadfastness, constancy, perseverance
more: n. m. S. निर्बाह 1. Execution; accomplishment; discharge. 2. Maintenance; livelihood. 3. Accommodation. Ek ghar meṅ do kā nibāh mushkil hai . 4. Constancy; perseverance. Log kahte haiṅ chāh mushkil hai, Sach to yeh hai nibāh mushkil hai .
s. m. 1. Accomplishment, performance, sufficiency, supply, maintenance. 2. Performing an engagement, keeping, guarding.
subs: (-kā), To carry through, carry into effect, to accomplish, &c.; to keep faith (with, -se); — to support, maintain; to protect; — to bear, put up (with), make the best (of, -kā); to pass time (with, -se), live in peace (with).

ety: nibāh˚ = Prk. निव्वाह इ) or निव्वाहे(इ) = S. निर्वाहय(ति), caus. of rt. निर्+वह्
def: v.t. To carry on or through, to conduct, manage, &c. (see nibāh, and nibāh karnā); — to take care of, to protect, guard; to support, maintain; — v. int. To keep faith (with); to deal (with), to act or behave (towards); — to afford; — to wear, last, endure.
more: v. a. 1. To accomplish, achieve, perfect, perform. 2. To protect, to guard, to take care of. 3. To keep oneʼs faith. 4. To behave. 5. To afford. 6. To conduct.

def: v.n. = nibhnā, q.v. v.n. To be accomplished, or performed, or effected; to succeed; — to serve, do, pass; to live, subsist, eke out a livelihood; to last, continue, endure.
v. a. 1. To accomplish; fulfil; carry into effect; discharge. 2. To keep faith with. 3. To support; maintain; protect. gale paṛe kā nibāhnā , v. a. To make the best of a bad bargain.
adj. Sufficient for (one's) purpose; enough to support, or maintain, &c.; — durable, lasting, permanent.
more: adj. Lasting, permanent, sufficient for oneʼs purpose.

def: s.m. One who carries on, or conducts, or manages, &c.; accomplisher, &c. (see nibāhnā).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Unfortunate, luckless; — unfortunate wretch, luckless wight.
more: n. An unfortunate wretch. Ae jān! Lakhnau se nikal jāūṅgī maiṅab , Wom. Jān Sāhab. Auqāt mujh nibakhti kī hotī basar nahīṅ .

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Unfortunate, luckless; — unfortunate wretch, luckless wight.
adj. Powerless, impotent, weak, feeble; — inferior; of little value (e.g. nibal sibal sab khapā-jātā hai).
more: adj. 1. Weak ( kamzor ). Jabar kī joë mahtārī hoë, nibal kī joë merī sārī hoë . Prov. The strong man's wife as a mother I respect, the weak man's wife I jest with as a sister-in-law. 2. Of little value; inferior ( ghaṭyal , 1). Nibal sibal sab khapā jātā hai . Both good and bad come into use. (Said by shop-keepers).

ety: nibal, q.v.+Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Powerlessness, impotence, weakness, prostration of strength (i.q. nirbalāʼī).

s.m. Binding, fastening, tying; attachment; composing; — restraint, confinement, obstruction; constipation; suppression of urine, strangury; — a band, bond, fetter; fixed property, a grant (of property); — a literary composition, a treatise.
adj. Fixed, determined, settled; affixed; ascertained (syn. ṭhahrāyā).
adj. Affixed, ascertained.

s.m. A binding, fastening, tie, ligation; building, construction; checking, restraining; restraint, confinement; — band, fetter; — ratification; — a composition; (in Gram.) syntax; — firmness; — that to which anything is fastened, or on which it rests; a receptacle; key, or peg (of a lute, &c.); — cause, origin; reason, motive; condition.
s.f. Band, bond, fetter.
adj. (f. -inī), Binding; confining; — joined, or connected (by, or with), bound (to); hanging together; — causing, being a cause (of).
part. (f. -ā), Bound, fastened, tied; confined.
s.m. (dialec.) Settlement, adjustment, decision; — passing, passage, transit; getting through, deliverance.
more: s. m. (dakh. i. q. گذاره) Passage, passing.

def: s.m. = نوارن niwāraṇ, q.v. s.m. Keeping back or off (from), defending; protection; setting free (from); removal (of); release, liberation; — prohibiting, prohibition; preventing, prevention; preclusion; interruption; exclusion; impediment, obstruction; opposing, opposition
subs: v.t. To keep back or off (from, -se), to defend, protect, &c.

def: s.m. Separation; deciding, decision, adjustment. settlement, disposal; accomplishing, accomplishment; ending, cessation, &c.

v.t. = نوارنا niwārnā, q.v. v.t. To surround; to keep off, ward off; to exclude, to shut out; to protect, defend; — to prohibit, forbid, interdict; — to prevent, hinder, impede, obstruct, interrupt; to check, restrain; to oppose.
more: v. a. 1. To forbid. 2. (dakh.) See نباڙنا nibāṛnā.

ety: nibāṛ˚ = Prk. निव्वाड(इ) or निव्वाडे(इ) = S. निर्वाटय(ति), caus. of rt. निर्+वट्
def: v.t. To separate, divide; to adjust, settle, decide; to accomplish, perform, manage, fulfil, carry through, keep (an engagement, &c.); to end; to spend, to consume.
more: v. a. 1. ( निर्वाहण ) To accomplish, to perform, manage: see نباهنا nibāhnā. 2. ( निर्व्वाण ) To spend, to end. 3. To separate.

ety: nibaṛ˚ = Prk. निव्वड(इ) or निव्वडे(इ); S. निर्वटय(ति), rt. निर् + वट्; — or nibaṛnā = nibaṭnā = nipaṭnā, q.v.
def: v.n. To be separated, or divided; to be settled, be adjusted, be decided; to be accomplished; to be completed, or finished; to be ended, be spent, be consumed
more: ( निर्वहण or intrans. of نباڙنا nibāṛnā ) v. n. To be accomplished, to be spent, to be ended.

def: v. n. 1. To be accomplished, completed. 2. To be spent. Rupayā nibaṛ gayā .
v.n. = nipaṭnā, and nibaṛnā, qq.v. v.t. To throw or cast down; to fell, to knock down, beat down; to overthrow, kill, destroy.

v. n. See nibṭānā , v. a. Jahāṅ bahū kā pīsnā vahīṅ susar kī khāṭ, Nibṭat āre pīsnā, sarkat āre khāt . Rus. song. Where she her corn doth grind her father-in-law hath there his bed, As to an end her work speeds on his bed is nearer led. nibṭī huī yā chhaṭī huī raqam , A rare scoundrel. jhagṛe kā nibṭerā , n. m. Settlement of an affair.
s.m. Stating, representing, or relating (to a superior), representation (syn. ʻarẓ), an address, a petition (i.q. nivedan)
more: n. m. S. निवेदन A petition ( prārthnā ).
s. m. An address, a petition, representation to a superiour.
subs: To make representation, to represent, to state (respectfully); to make application, to plead.

def: s.m. corr. of nibedan, or nivedan, qq.v. s.m. Stating, representing, or relating (to a superior), representation (syn. ʻarẓ), an address, a petition (i.q. nivedan)
more: s. m. A petition, representation. See نبيدن nibedan.
subs: To make representation, to represent, to state (respectfully); to make application, to plead.

ety: fr. nibehnā, a dialec. form of nibāhnā, q.v.
def: adj. (dialec.) Uninterrupted, continuous, continual, unceasing, constant, lasting, permanent (i.q. nibāhū, q.v.).
s.m. Accomplishment, finishing; finish, end; settlement, adjustment, decision (i.q. nibṭerā).
s.f. (dialec.) Accomplishment; finishing; settlement, &c. (i.q. nibeṛā; nibṭerā); — a young woman (syn. yuvatī).

ety: nibeṛ˚ = Prk. निव्वंड(इ) = S. निर्वण्ड(ते), rt. निर्+वण्ड्; cf. nibāṛnā, of which nibeṛnā is another form
def: v.t. To separate, divide; to accomplish; to settle, adjust, decide; to complete, finish, terminate, put an end to; to spend, consume
more: or see نباڙنا nibāṛnā ) v. a. To end, to put an end to, complete, perform, finish, spend.

def: adj. & s.m. Accomplishing, performing, finishing, &c.; — accomplisher, performer, finisher; adjuster, decider, &c.
adj. (f. -ā), Like, resembling, similar (only occurring as last member of compounds; sometimes pleonastically after adj.).
adj. (f. -ī), Unlucky, unfortunate; poor (also nirbhāgī).
more: Mah. adj. S. निर्भाग. Unfortunate; poor ( bad-bakht ).

def: adj. Unfortunate.

v.t. = نباهنا nibāhnā, q.v. v.t. To carry on or through, to conduct, manage, &c. (see nibāh, and nibāh karnā); — to take care of, to protect, guard; to support, maintain; — v. int. To keep faith (with); to deal (with), to act or behave (towards); — to afford; — to wear, last, endure.
more: (causal of نبهنا) v. a. To perform, to guard, &c. See نباهنا nibāhnā.
wety: Ultimately from Sanskrit निर्वाहयति (nirvāhayati). Compare Punjabi ਨਿਭਾਉਣਾ (nibhāuṇā) / نِبھاؤݨا (nibhā'oṇā), Sindhi نِڀائِڻُ (nibhāiṇu) / निभाइणु, Gujarati નિભાવવું (nibhāvvũ), Marathi निभावणे (nibhāvṇe).

def: s.m. Accomplishing, accomplishment, &c. (i.q. nibāh, q.v.); — continuance; permanence; durability.
s. (dakh.) Guarding, accomplishment.
v. a. (dakh.) See نباڙنا nibāṛnā, and نباهنا nibāhnā.
v.n. To be accomplished, or performed, or effected; to succeed; — to serve, do, pass; to live, subsist, eke out a livelihood; to last, continue, endure.
more: v. n. S. निर्बहण See نبهانا
v. n. To serve, succeed, pass, live, last, be accomplished or perfected or performed.
v. a. To continue, to endure.

def: adj. (dialec.) Without dignity, or respect; disgraced, dishonoured; worthless.

adj. = niviḍ, q.v. adj. Without spaces or interstices; close, contiguous; thick, gross, dense, impervious (as a forest, or darkness, &c.); — coarse; — heavy, hard (as rain, &c.); sound (sleep); — firm; — large, bulky; — crooked-nosed; — s.m. A crooked-nosed person; — name of a mountain.
v.n. (colloq.) = nibhnā, q.v. v.n. To be accomplished, or performed, or effected; to succeed; — to serve, do, pass; to live, subsist, eke out a livelihood; to last, continue, endure.
s.f. The fruit or berry of the nīm tree (also niṅbaulī; rustic nibaurī).
more: n. f. The fruit of the nīm tree. 1. Nīm kī nibaulī kaṛvī, Sāvan bhī kabhī āvegā? Jīo, rī, merī mā kā jāyā! gāṛī bhej bulāvegā . Hin. Girls' song. Nīm fruit is bitter, when will Sāvan rains descend? O may my brother live! a cart for me when will he send? 2. Ek nār tarvar se utrī, māṅ se janam na pāyo, Bāp kā nām jo vā se pūchho ādhā nām batāyo! Ādhā nām batāyo, Khusrau, kaun mulak kī bolī? Ber ber jo vā se pūchho apnā nām na-bolī . Riddle. Ans. Nibolī. The fruit of the Nīm . No mother gave her birth, she came down from a tree, When asked but half her father's name disclose would she. But half one's name to give, where doth such strange speech dwell? Though asked repeatedly her own name she'd not tell. 3. Nibaurī khāne se āṅkheṅ nahīn dukhtīṅ . Eat nīm fruit and it will take away the pain in your eyes.

def: v. a. S. निवृत See bhugtānā . nibṭā huā ; Sant. gākhuṛ , n. m. An experienced person.
v.t. To settle, adjust, decide, &c. (see nipṭānā)
s.m. Determination, settlement, &c. (i.q. nipṭāʼo, and nibṭerā, qq.v.).
n. m. 1. Accomplishment; completion. 2. Settlement; adjustment; discharge.
s.m. Settlement, adjustment, determination, decision, discharge; accomplishment, completion.
more: s. m. Determination. See نٿارا niṭārā.

def: s.m. The lime, Citrus medica (tree and fruit; — also nimbū, nīṅbū, nīmū, līmū, lembū)
more: n. m. S. निम्बू P. līmū . 1. A lime, and also the tree; Citrus medica . Wat. Kāgzī nībū haiṅ ras achār ko! Delhi Street cry. 2. A young woman's breasts. nībū-nichoṛ , n. m. 1. A lime-squeezer. 2. A self-invited guest; a loafer.
s. Name of a tree, a lime. (Citrus medica). See ليمو līmū.
subs: s.m. One who expresses lime-juice; — (met.) a self-invited guest; a loafer.

ety: nibuk˚ = S. निर्मुक्त, p.p.p. of निर्+मुच्
def: v.t. (dialec.) To loosen, to set free, liberate; to separate; to sunder, disjoin (syn. ćhuṛānā); — v.n. To come out (of); to be loosed, or set free (from), to escape, &c. (i.q. nikalnā).

ety: Prk. नीचो, or नीचं; S. नीचः, or नीचं
def: adj. Low; situated in a low position; deep (down); — short, small, dwarfish; — low, vile, base, mean; petty, insignificant, inferior
more: adj. S. नीच See اُوچها 7. Vile. 1. Lālach sīs nivāt hai, lālach sīs kaṭāë, Lālach sūṅ, re bālke, uttam nīch kahāë . Rus. 'Tis greed that bows the head, for greed men lose their lives, 'Tis greed, hear me, my child, the great to baseness drives. 2. Nīch na chhoṛe nichāī, nīm na chhoṛe titāī . Prov. The base their baseness cant resign, nor nīm its bitterness. 3. Nīchān kūṭān, devān pūjāṅ . Prov. The low are crushed, gods are worshipped. (One man may take away a horse, while another may not look over a hedge). nīch zāt yā jāt , n. f. 1. Low caste. 2. A low caste man. Nīch-zātoṅ meṅ ab bhī baṛā ekā hai . Prov. Saying. In low castes still the bond of union doth survive.
adj. Low, base, vile, mean, vulgar. نيچ اونچ nīch-ūnch ( नीचोच्च ) s. f. 1. Inequality. 2. The ups and downs of life.
subs: s.f. The high and the low; — inequalities; unevenness; — the ups and downs (of life)
or nīć-zāt, s.f. Low caste; the lower orders; — a man of low caste
s.m. (f. -in, or nī), A goldsmith or jeweller of low caste
s.f. Low or vile earnings, ill-gotten gain
vulg. nīćag, adj. (f. -ā), Going downwards, descending (as a river); — being in, or belonging to, a low man, or a low class of men; low, vile, base
s.m. (in Astrol.) The house or station of the heavens in which a planet stands at its lowest point
s.m. (dialec.) A low-caste barber; an inferior sort of barber.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Low (in situation or position); depressed, &c. (i.q. nīć, q.v.)
more: adj. Low; depressed. Bamnā! bar ḍhūṅḍ barobar ko! Jo, bamnā, bar ūṅchak, nīchak, to sabar paṛego mere joban ko! Rus. Wom. song. Brahman! a spouse in years my equal, pray secure! An older or a younger would my breasts try sore. nīchā alag , ( Bot .) Inferior, or free. nīchā ūṅchā dekhnā , To look at a thing all round; to ponder well. nīchā paṛnā , To be humbled, subdued. nīchā dikhānā , v. a. To disgrace; dishonor. nīchā dekhnā , v. n. To be dishonored, disgraced.

(see نيچ) adj. Below, down, low. نيچا اونچا nīchā ūnchā, Up and down, uneven ground, abrupt. نيچي سرون سي بولنا nīche suroṅ se bolnā, To sing with a low voice, or hum a tune. (Jocosè autem crepitus ventris hâcce voce subintelligitur).
subs: adj. (f. nīćī-ūṅćī), Up and down, high and low, uneven, not level or equal; abrupt
(-kā), To look high and low, or at the top and bottom (of); to look at (a thing) all round; to scan minutely; to ponder well
v.n. To fall low; to come below; — to be subdued or humbled
v.n. 'To look low'; to be disgraced, or dishonoured
(obl. of nīćā; Prk. nīće; S. nīće), postp. & adv. Below, under, beneath, at the bottom (of, -ke); down, below
v.n. To come down, to descend, to dismount, &c.; to fall down; — to come under
adv. Up and down; — one above and the other below; — upside-down, topsy-turvy
v.n. To go down, to descend; to subside; to submit, to succumb
To sing with a low voice, to hum (a tune); — jocose autem crepitus ventris hacce voce subintelligitur
From bottom to top; from head to foot
v.t. To lower; to hang down (the head); to degrade; to abase
v.t. To throw or hurl down; to knock down
To bend the neck, or hang down the head (from shame or humility)
wety: Derived from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀡𑀺𑀘𑁆𑀘 (ṇicca) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit नीच (nīcá), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *niHćás (“facing down”), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *niHčás, from Proto-Indo-European *ni-h₃kʷ-ós, from *ní (“down”) + *-h₃kʷ- (“facing, looking”).

ety: nīćā, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Lowness; depth; declivity, &c. (i.q. nićān, q.v.); humbleness, inferiority, &c. ( = nīć-pan, q.v.).
s.f. = nićāʼī, the com. form, q.v. s.f. Lowness; depth; declivity, &c. (i.q. nićān, q.v.); humbleness, inferiority, &c. ( = nīć-pan, q.v.).

ety: nīća or nīćā, q.v.+lā = Prk. ल्लओ, or लओ = S. ल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Lower, nether, under; — s.m. The lower one, the one underneath.
more: adj. Quiet; silent. Nichlā baiṭh daṅgā mat kar!

ety: Prk. निच्चलअं; S. निश्चल+कं, rt. चल् with निस्
def: adj. & adv. Motionless, still, quiet (e.g. nićlā baiṭh daṅgā mat kar).

ety: nīćā, q.v.+ān = Ap. Prk. अणु = Prk. अणं (for त्तणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: adj. (f. -kī), Low, depressed (ground, &c.); situated in a hollow, or in a valley, &c.
more: n. m. nichāī , n. f. S. नीच A slope; declivity.
(from नीच see نيچ nīch ) s. (dakh.) A cavity, hollow, bottom, valley. ( Harris ).

def: s.m. & f. = nićān, the com. form, q.v. adj. (f. -kī), Low, depressed (ground, &c.); situated in a hollow, or in a valley, &c.
v.t. = nićuṛwānā, q.v.
v.n. (dialec.) = nićaṛnā, q.v.
more: v. n. 1. To be squeezed, pressed. 2. To drop; trickle; ooze. Pānī nichaṛnā .

ety: nićaṛ˚ = Prk. णिञ्चल(इ) = S. निश्चर(ति), rt. निस्+चर्
def: v.n. To flow out gently, to trickle, ooze, drop, distil; — to be squeezed or pressed; to be wrung (see nićoṛnā).
v.t. To cause to flow, or trickle, &c.; — to have or get (water, &c.) wrung out, or strained, &c.; — to cause to wring, or press, or strain, &c.

ety: nīćā, q.v.+ās = wās = wāns = wāṅćh = Prk. वंछा = S. वाञ्छा
def: s.f. Lowness, &c. (i.q. nićān, and nićāʼī, qq.v.); lowliness, humbleness; inferiority; baseness, &c. (i.q. nīć-pan, q.v.).
(prob. akin to nićaṛnā, q.v.), s.f.? (dialec.) = viśesh, q.v.
s.f. = S نيچتو नीचत्व nīćatva, s.m. = nīćpan, q.v.
s.m. = S نيچتا नीचता nīćatā, s.f. = nīćpan, q.v.

def: s.m. Piling or heaping up; — a collection, heap, multitude, quantity, store, stock; an assemblage.

def: s.m. Name of one of the degraded castes sprung from the outcast or Vrātya Kshatriyas.
adv. See s.v. nīćā. adj. (f. -ī), Low (in situation or position); depressed, &c. (i.q. nīć, q.v.)
more: adv. Below; down; under; beneath. 1. Nīche se to itnā sā, aur pher phār bahūterā re, Jab mārūṅ tab 'bam bam' bole: būjh pahelā morā re! Riddle. Ans. Naqqāra . A kettledrum. Its depth is small, its girth great round about, Beat it, it cries ' bam bam' —canst make this out? 2. Zāt ke bulāë barābar baiṭhe, kam-zāt bulāë nïche baiṭhe . Prov. Caste-brethren guests all equal sit, but low-caste guests low places fit. nīche ūpar , 1. Up and down. 2. One below or above the other. 3. In the act; in coitu . nīche sur meṅ . In a low key. nīche se , adv. From below. nīche se ūpar tak , adv. From head to foot. nīche kā dhaṛ , n. m. 1. The lower part of the body from the loins downwards. 2. The privities. nīche-kī , 1. Bottom. 2. The vulva or fundament. nīche kī adālat , n. f. A lower court. nīchī nazroṅ se . With downcast looks. nīche lānā , v. a. To bring under (Wrestling). nīchī nāṛ k. , v. a. To ha…
subs: adj. (f. nīćī-ūṅćī), Up and down, high and low, uneven, not level or equal; abrupt
(-kā), To look high and low, or at the top and bottom (of); to look at (a thing) all round; to scan minutely; to ponder well
v.n. To fall low; to come below; — to be subdued or humbled
v.n. 'To look low'; to be disgraced, or dishonoured
(obl. of nīćā; Prk. nīće; S. nīće), postp. & adv. Below, under, beneath, at the bottom (of, -ke); down, below
v.n. To come down, to descend, to dismount, &c.; to fall down; — to come under
adv. Up and down; — one above and the other below; — upside-down, topsy-turvy
v.n. To go down, to descend; to subside; to submit, to succumb
To sing with a low voice, to hum (a tune); — jocose autem crepitus ventris hacce voce subintelligitur
From bottom to top; from head to foot
v.t. To lower; to hang down (the head); to degrade; to abase
v.t. To throw or hurl down; to knock down
To bend the neck, or hang down the head (from shame or humility)

def: s.f. corr. of nićintāʼī, q.v. s.f. Freedom from care or anxiety, &c.; leisure; thoughtlessness, heedlessness, unconcern, fearlessness.
adj. = niśćeshṭ, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Deprived of motion; incapable of motion or effort; motionless; powerless, helpless.
s. f. Lowness.
adj. Unadulterated; untainted; pure, genuine; free from flaw or defect, &c.
more: W. adj. 1. Pure (انميل 1, 2). 2. Free from flaw or crack.

def: Caus. of nichoṛnā q. v.

def: s.m. A propitiatory offering, a sacrifice; a victim; — money which is scattered at marriages and on other festive occasions (i.q. nis̤ār, q.v.); a present or offering (to a great personage); — the price paid for an idol
more: n. f. 1. A propitiatory offering (نثار). Mujhe Khudā de to maiṅ apne bachche kī shādi meṅ motī nichhāvar karūṅ! Mah. W.om. 2. The price paid for an idol.
s. f. A propitiatory offering, sacrifice, victim.

def: adj. (dakh.) Pure, clear (water).
s. f. Carelessness, fearlessness, thoughtlessness, unconcern, leisure.
adj. Free from thought, or anxiety, or care; thoughtless, void of reflection, inconsiderate, heedless, unconcerned, careless; — at leisure
more: adj. S. निश्चिन्त Pālī, nichchitto . Free from thought, anxiety, or care; unconcerned; at leisure. nichant baiṭhnā yā h. , v. n. To be free from care, at leisure, or at one's ease.
adj. Free from thought, anxiety, or care, unconcerned, careless, at leisure. نچنت هونا nichint honā, To be at leisure, to be disengaged, to have finished.
subs: or nićint honā or ho-jānā (-se), To be free from care or anxiety (in respect of), to be at leisure, be disengaged; to be unconcerned (about), &c.

def: s.m. That which is wrung or pressed out; expressed juice (of); extract; outcome; essence, substance, gist, pith; — end, termination (of an affair).
more: s. m. 1. The end or termination of any affair. 2. The burthen, or that on which any thing depends.

def: v.t. prop. نچڙانا nićuṛānā = نچڙوانا nićaṛwānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to flow, or trickle, &c.; — to have or get (water, &c.) wrung out, or strained, &c.; — to cause to wring, or press, or strain, &c.
s.m. = nićoṛwā, q.v. adj. & s.m. Exacting, extortionate, rapacious; niggardly; — one who wrings (anything) out of, an extortioner; a plunderer; — a niggard, miser.
v.t. (dialec.) = nićoṛnā, q.v.
more: v. a. 1. To wring; squeeze. 2. To milk; bleed. 1. Ab-ke kāl ne to sab ko nichoṛ līyā . 2. Sāṅvan mās baho banvāṅ, ban phūl gayo apni rut māṅ, Kāhu chātur nāri ke hāth paro, jin bīn baṭor dharo chhat māṅ Luṛhyā pe luṛho, dhunyā ke dhuno dhun pe dhar-ke. Kāhu chātur nār ke hāth paro, jin kāt bunāë taiyār karo, Korī kī āṅṭ kī sāṅt sahi, dhobi kī choṭ sahī sil kī, Raṅg-rej ne bor nichoṛ dharo, darjī ne tān dharo gaj pe, Itne dukh pāë bano kapṛā, tab kyoṅ na kilol kure kuch pe? In August but a seed, in time to cotton I swelled out, Next in a clever woman's hands picked, gathered, and spread out, Pressed in a mill, then carded by the carder on his bow, Spun by a clever woman's hands and woven I was now, By weaver tortured, and by laundress beaten 'gainst a stone, By dyer soaked and wrung, then stretched the tailor's yard upon, Cloth wrought with pains so great should I not toy with breasts I'v…

ety: caus. of nićuṛnā, a dialec. form of nićaṛnā; — nićoṛ˚ = nićāṛ˚ = Prk. निच्चाले(इ) = S. निश्चारय(ति), caus. of rt. निस्+चर्
def: v.t. 'To cause to issue or come forth'; to press or squeeze, to wring; to press out, to express; to strain; to wring out, to extort, exact; — to milk
more: v. a. To wring, squeeze, press, strain, express, extort, to exact.
v. a. (dakh.) To squeeze out (milk). دود نچوڙنا dūd nichoṛnā, To milk.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit *𑀦𑀺𑀘𑁆𑀘𑁄𑀟𑁂𑀤𑀺 (*niccoḍedi), from Sanskrit *निश्चोटयति (*niścoṭayati), from निस्- (nis-, “out”) + चुट् (cuṭ, “to cut off”).
